column65: For the social it would have been essential to have a proper dinner. Coming straight from the conference with only snacks provided made me leave the social earlier than I otherwise would have in the search of a proper meal.

Long lunch breaks with poster sessions were a great idea. 
Great talk variety and mix of biology/computer science. It was great to invite e.g. CL who is a biologist and brought his perspectives to the table. 
The food for lunch break was not great - especially the first day there was no choice, very little portions, no food during other breaks except for biscuits. Some more variety would be nice.

column4: Cell/Molecular Biology;Chemistry/Biochemistry;Image Analysis/Processing;Software Engineering;Computer Vision;Machine Learning;

column65: Do not like vegetarian food. The pieces of salmon were tiny )))
There was a bit of chaos with the posters

This is a general comment and something a colleague and myself were chatting about recently. The bioimaging/image analysis seems to be very much limited to cell/tissue/developmental biology (appreciate this is the main research focus at the Crick), despite there being huge amounts of research utilising other forms of bioimage analysis, especially in other areas of animal biology (for example recording behaviour of animals, aspects of animal physiology), and in the methods of bioimaging (for example using drones/automated videoing equipment to phenotype plants, or look at species distributions etc). We both agreed that it would be really great to see a wider representation of bioimage analysts across areas of biology, as we all ultimately use very similar approaches, and might learn a lot from eachother. 

Just my 10 cents, but otherwise it was a great conference, with really good representation from ECRs and lots of opportunities to chat to new people. Thanks!

column4: Cell/Molecular Biology;Developmental Biology;Image Analysis/Processing;Computer Vision;Machine Learning;

column65: I would appreciate access to posters (maybe online format) for virtual attendees.
I did not like the lunch options on the 2nd day at all. Receiving a menu beforehand would be nice.

The organisers did an amazing job!
Thank you
Community talk by Tom was great - might be good to work towards some of his suggestions for CBIAS 2025
Thanks for organizing!
Improve food quantity and more speakers selected from the abstracts.
The lunches were a bit too difficult to eat without tables!  Either more tables or sandwiches/finger food would be better.
There wasn't enough Vegan food at the even social, and the canapees were too small to be considered a full meal. But, everything else was great.

column4: Image Analysis/Processing;Computer Science;Computer Vision;Software Engineering;Machine Learning;

column65: Napari workshop online
I would like to have more talks about actual image analysis and less about community initiatives. At times it was just long lists of groups and initiatives without much content.

To help with travel and accommodation costs for participants, I was wondering if you could provide some bursaries or travel grants for us to apply for at future events? Or any registration discounts would also be helpful.

Thank you again for organising this wonderful symposium and I look forward to attending the next one somewhere (maybe not in Crick as mentioned during the event).
It was a well organized symposium with good talks. The organizers demonstrated great ability in throwing the microphone.

I think it was an overall great symposium, but the only bioimaging represented was cellular based sciences. Imaging is used in other strata of biology, such as ecology and evolution - they use varied an interesting analytical techniques that would be interesting to add to the conference.

column65: Please ensure that a proper dinner is provided at the social event. This made me leave early because I needed to go get dinner at a restaurant.
